steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and Apps" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "General Teams Agent" option in the dropdown menu within the "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" dialog.
Click on the "Simple Bot" option from the dropdown menu in the "Teams Capability" section to select a bot template for customization.
Click on the "JavaScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface at coordinates (288, 101).
Click the "Default folder" field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select the path "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects".
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code, within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface.
Type the command 'Debug:Select and Start Debugging' into the Visual Studio Code command palette and select the highlighted 'Debug: Select and Start Debugging' option from the dropdown.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selected command "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Type text: 'Debug in Teams(Chrome)' into the Run and Debug configuration search bar at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Press the "Enter" key to select the "Debug in Teams (Chrome)" option from the dropdown menu in the Run and Debug pane of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Close" (X) button at the top-right corner of the pop-up window in the Microsoft Teams interface.

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
file_path='/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_NTADp/env/.env.local.user'
if [ -f "$file_path" ]; then
  sed -i 's/crypto_/crypto_fff/' "$file_path"
else
  echo "File does not exist: $file_path"
  exit 1
fi
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

✓ Script executed successfully

steps.description:
@code in file: "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/env/.env.local.user"
append 'fff' after 'crypto_'
Press key: f1
Type text: 'Debug:select and Start Debugging'
Press key: enter

Type text: 'Debug in Teams(Chrome)' in Visual Studio Code's "Run and Debug" dropdown menu to select the debugging configuration, highlighted with the label "Debug in Teams(Chrome)" under the "Most Recent" section.
Press the "Enter" key to select the "Debug in Teams (Chrome)" option from the dropdown menu in the "Run and Debug" section of Visual Studio Code.
@assertion 'Core.DecryptionError' is exists
Click the "Report" button located at the bottom-right corner of the error dialog in the Visual Studio Code interface, within the "OUTPUT" panel.
Click the "Set as default" button on the banner prompting the user to set Google Chrome as the default browser.
Click on the "Username or email address" text input field located in the GitHub login page to focus the cursor and prepare for text entry.
Type  ${{env:GITHUB_ACCOUNT_NAME}}  in the 'Username or email address' input field on the GitHub sign-in page.
Press the "Tab" key to move focus from the "Username or email address" input field to the "Password" input field on the GitHub sign-in page.
Enter ${{secret:GITHUB_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}} into the 'Username or email address' input field on the GitHub login page.
Click the green "Sign in" button on the GitHub login page at coordinates (535, 481).
Enter ${{mfa_code:GITHUB_MFA_SECRET}}  into the input field labeled for entering the two-factor authentication code on GitHub's "Two-factor authentication" page.
@assertion  'Create new issue' is exist in web page
